seoTitle: Newton Boyd vs Niagara Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Newton Boyd and Niagara Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Newton Boyd vs Niagara Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Newton Boyd and Niagara Park.
heading: Newton Boyd vs Niagara Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Newton Boyd, New South Wales and Niagara Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Newton Boyd has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Niagara Park (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Newton Boyd or Niagara Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Newton Boyd has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Niagara Park scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Newton Boyd scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Niagara Park scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Newton Boyd or Niagara Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Newton Boyd has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
